summary: mcp-server-git name-squat exfiltrates installer host metadata via postinstall
details: This package squats the unscoped name `mcp-server-git` and ships a postinstall lifecycle hook that runs index.js on every `npm install`. index.js (lines 14-29) requires `os`/`https`/`http`, then POSTs a JSON body containing `os.hostname()`, `process.cwd()`, `process.env.npm_config_user_agent`, `os.platform()`, the Node version, and a timestamp to the hardcoded endpoint `https://npx-canary-log.vulnerable-live.workers.dev/log`. package.json declares `"scripts": { "postinstall": "node index.js" }`, so the network beacon fires automatically on default install for any developer or CI system that resolves this name (including transitive resolution). Installer-side host identifiers leave the machine without consent or opt-out, regardless of whether the author frames the project as a canary/research exercise. The destination is an author-controlled Cloudflare Workers endpoint, not a vendor-published telemetry service, and there is no disclosure or opt-out mechanism in the install flow.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
